title: Rustic Irish Pork Chops Skillet
description: A tangy and chewy Irish dish featuring marinated pork chops, this recipe is sure to evoke nostalgic feelings. It is made with ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients and served on a talavera plate.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together apple cider vinegar, honey, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the pork chops to the marinade and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large talavera plate or oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the pork chops from the marinade and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned.
Transfer the pork chops to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil and saute the sliced onions for 5 minutes, or until they start to caramelize.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes to the skillet and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Return the pork chops to the skillet and transfer to the oven.
Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 20-25 minutes, or until the pork chops reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Let the pork chops rest for 5 minutes before serving.
